commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] No repair lane is needed because the latest head addresses the prior matcher finding; maintainers should use normal PR merge and check gates.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain concern found; the diff adds a metadata flag, matcher logic, tests, and a changelog entry without new dependencies, workflows, secrets, or install scripts. (sha=dbb8eb28d89061ee0ca8b5a119ee797b1614b268)
commands.review_summary:
The branch threads a generated-bundle marker from browser bundle creation into ChatGPT pre-send attachment readiness, relaxes stem-only matching for those bundles, and adds regression tests plus a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection, though I did not run a live browser reproduction in this read-only review. Current main lacks the generated-bundle stem fallback, and the PR body includes real ChatGPT terminal/CDP evidence for the reported chip shapes.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No repair lane is needed because the latest head addresses the prior matcher finding; maintainers should use normal PR merge and check gates.

target.title: Relax generated bundle attachment readiness
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes a browser-mode attachment readiness edge case for Oracle-generated browser bundles.

ChatGPT may expose `attachments-bundle.txt` / `.zip` as an extensionless `attachments-bundle` tile. The pre-send gate now accepts that only when Oracle explicitly marked the attachment as a generated bundle; normal user files retain exact or extension-aware matching.

## Changes

- Carry explicit generated-bundle metadata from bundle creation into local and remote submission readiness.
- Preserve current main's duplicate-renamed and ellipsized filename matching.
- Accept extensionless stems only for generated bundles.
- Recognize current ChatGPT file tiles, which expose filenames through `role="group"` / `aria-label` without an attachment testid.
- Reject embedded names, wrong extensions, duplicate-renamed wrong extensions, and user-owned files that merely share the generated bundle basename.
- Add focused regressions and an `0.13.1 — Unreleased` changelog entry with contributor credit.

## Authenticated browser proof

Verified June 11, 2026 against a real signed-in ChatGPT profile using harmless local content:

- Uploaded `attachments-bundle.txt`; ChatGPT rendered the real tile as `attachments-bundle(1).txt`.
- The production readiness expression returned `true`.
- On that live tile, temporarily reproduced the extensionless artifact shape with no file input or remove affordance: generated-bundle readiness returned `true`, while the same basename treated as a user file returned `false`.
- Restored the DOM before submission, sent `Reply exactly ORACLE_PR240_BROWSER_OK.`, and received `ORACLE_PR240_BROWSER_OK`.
- Account details and conversation URL are intentionally omitted.

The contributor's June 3 proof also completed a generated-bundle browser send with `ORACLE_BUNDLE_PROOF_OK`.

## Validation

- `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `pnpm vitest run tests/browser/promptComposerExpressions.test.ts tests/browser/prompt.test.ts tests/browser/promptComposer.test.ts tests/browser/attachmentsCompletion.test.ts` — 64 passed
- `pnpm test` — 1,135 passed, 41 skipped
- `pnpm run check`
- `pnpm run build`
- `pnpm run docs:check`
- `pnpm run test:packed-cli`
- `/Users/steipete/Projects/agent-skills/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main --engine codex --model gpt-5.5 --thinking high --stream-engine-output` — clean, no accepted/actionable findings
